rationale.R1.2: Methodological reasoning has identifiable gaps — kernel selection is justified only by 'prior experience' with no elaboration, and 'agreement' in the 89% benchmark is undefined; tightening these logical links is a natural expectation of draft improvement on a methods section.
rationale.R1.3: The 89% recovery rate is asserted as 'reasonable' without specifying the tolerance criterion, the comparison metric, or how heterogeneity was controlled; aligning this claim to its evidentiary basis is expected under a general 'improve' instruction on scientific prose.

rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' most directly signals writing-quality work; the text exhibits pervasive run-on sentences (paragraph 3 is a single sentence with five conjunctions), redundant nominalization ('the detrending step uses'), and weak hedging ('appears to be relatively robust') — these are the load-bearing targets of the delegation.
